# DaytonGrowthCo.

> DaytonGrowthCo builds custom business tools, automations, websites, and digital systems for small and midsized businesses. The company uses AI-assisted development to deliver custom-fit work at a fraction of traditional custom development cost.

Last updated: 2026-07-07

## Entity Summary

DaytonGrowthCo is a Dayton, Ohio company founded by Samuel Caruso. It serves small and midsized businesses across the United States, including contractors, trades, home-service companies, professional offices, and local service organizations.

## Core Positioning

Small businesses often need custom tools, but traditional custom software is priced for larger companies. DaytonGrowthCo starts with the business process, configures existing tools when they fit, and builds custom tools when the workflow needs something specific. AI-assisted development reduces build time and cost without turning the work into a generic template.

## What DaytonGrowthCo Builds

- Phone agents that answer calls, collect details, schedule appointments, and send summaries.
- Quote calculators, pricing tools, and estimate builders using approved business rules.
- Project dashboards, staff dashboards, customer portals, and internal workflow tools.
- Training libraries, SOP portals, sales pages, proposal pages, product visuals, and custom apps.
- Modern websites, local SEO, answer engine optimization, and campaign pages.
- Practical automations that reduce repeated entry, missed handoffs, slow quoting, and scattered customer information.

## Best Fit Customers

- Contractors and trades
- Roofing, repair, landscaping, and other home-service businesses
- Professional offices and local service organizations
- Owner-operated teams that rely on spreadsheets, texts, PDFs, repeated manual entry, or informal handoffs
- Small and midsized businesses that need custom-fit tools without enterprise custom-development pricing

## Buying and Pricing Notes

- Websites start at $1,500.
- Custom tools and automations are custom quoted.
- AI-assisted development can reduce the hours a traditional custom development shop would bill for.
- DaytonGrowthCo looks first for the smallest useful fix: existing software setup, focused automation, or custom build.
- A project should have a clear business case based on time saved, errors avoided, capacity recovered, or revenue opportunities protected.
